findings.title: Inquiry form submits no data — onSubmit only calls preventDefault
findings.description: The form's onSubmit handler is `(e) => e.preventDefault()` with no state, no fetch, and no side-effects, so submitted inquiries are silently discarded. Wire up controlled inputs and POST the payload to an API route or third-party service (e.g. Formspree, a Next.js route handler), then show a success/error message to the user.

findings.title: No input validation on any form field before submission
findings.description: All five form fields (first name, last name, mobile, email, target location) are uncontrolled inputs with no `required` attribute, no pattern constraint, and no client-side validation logic — a blank or malformed form can be 'submitted' without feedback. Add `required` attributes at minimum, validate the `tel` field against a Philippine mobile pattern (e.g. `/^(\+?63|0)9\d{9}$/`), and validate email format before allowing submission.

findings.title: select options are missing value attributes — capital range is unreadable by code
findings.description: The four `<option>` elements for 'Available Capital' omit the `value` attribute, so any future submission logic would receive the display string (including peso symbols) rather than a clean programmatic key. Add explicit values such as `value="below_500k"`, `value="500k_1m"`, etc.

findings.title: Malformed CSS value in inline style — boxShadow contains a literal underscore
findings.description: `boxShadow: "0 4px_24px rgba(0,0,0,0.5)"` uses an underscore instead of a space between `4px` and `24px`, making the value invalid and silently ignored by the browser. Change it to `"0 4px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.5)"`.

findings.title: Hero stat '50+ Active Branches' is a hardcoded magic value with no source
findings.description: The branch count is a hardcoded string literal in a data array; if the real number changes it will silently fall out of date and mislead prospective franchisees. Move it to a shared constants file or a CMS field so it can be updated in one place, and note the source/date it was accurate.

findings.title: Story.tsx component renders as id='packages' section but is named Story — misleading nav anchor
findings.description: The `<section>` has `id="packages"` and the right-column cards container has `id="story"`, but the Navbar link labelled 'Our Story' points to `#story` which resolves to an inner `<div>`, not a top-level section — scroll behavior will be off and screen readers will land in the middle of a card list. Give the section its own correct `id="story"` and rename or remove the inner div id.

findings.title: Gallery image sizes attribute does not account for col-span-2 images
findings.description: All 12 images share `sizes="(max-width: 768px) 50vw, 25vw"` even though `sample1.jpg` spans 2 columns and `sample8.jpg` spans 2 columns — both should use `50vw` on desktop. This causes Next.js Image to generate and serve under-sized srcset candidates for those images. Set per-image `sizes` values matching their actual rendered width.

findings.title: MDI icon font loaded from CDN via layout.tsx head — no fallback or integrity hash
findings.description: The Material Design Icons stylesheet is injected via a `<link>` in the `<head>` with no `integrity` or `crossorigin` attribute; a CDN compromise or network failure will silently break all icon rendering. Add a Subresource Integrity hash (`integrity="sha384-..."`) and `crossOrigin="anonymous"`, or bundle the font locally.
